itunes_results.name: Merriam-Webster Dictionary
itunes_results.note: Dictionary with etymology notes — not daily etymology education

itunes_results.name: Dictionary.com
itunes_results.note: Reference tool — no streak, no curriculum, no history focus

daily_etymology_streak.gap_note: Etymonline (the gold standard etymology resource) is web-only with no app. No streak app teaches word origins systematically: PIE roots, Latin borrowings, French Norman conquest vocabulary shifts, Greek scientific terminology, how words evolved meaning. Merriam-Webster includes etymologies but buries them in definition lookups — no curriculum, no streak, no gamification.

itunes_results.name: Duolingo
itunes_results.note: Teaches modern spoken languages — zero historical linguistics content

itunes_results.name: Babbel
itunes_results.note: Modern language acquisition — no history of language science

daily_historical_linguistics_streak.gap_note: Historical linguistics (the scientific study of how languages change over time: sound shifts, Grimm's Law, Great Vowel Shift, Neogrammarian hypothesis, comparative method) has zero dedicated educational app. Duolingo and Babbel teach vocabulary; neither explains WHY English 'father' and Latin 'pater' and Sanskrit 'pitr' share a root, or how the entire Indo-European family was reconstructed from living languages.

itunes_results.name: Duolingo Latin
itunes_results.note: Has Latin course but as modern language learning, not cultural/historical appreciation

itunes_results.name: Lingua Latina
itunes_results.note: Small app, reading-focused — no streaks, no gamification, very low reach

daily_dead_languages_streak.gap_note: Latin, Ancient Greek, Sanskrit, Old English, Classical Nahuatl, Classical Arabic — dead languages with enormous intellectual prestige and millions of modern learners, yet no streak app exists that teaches their cultural context, literature, and importance in daily 2-minute lessons. Duolingo Latin reduces Latin to vocabulary drills; nobody teaches WHY these languages matter, what they reveal about ancient civilizations, and how they shaped modern thought.

itunes_results.name: Simply Chinese
itunes_results.note: Chinese character learning — not history of writing systems globally

itunes_results.name: Egyptian Hieroglyphics
itunes_results.note: Hieroglyphics reference — static, no streak, no historical context

daily_writing_systems_streak.gap_note: The invention of writing is one of humanity's most transformative events — cuneiform (3200 BCE), hieroglyphics, Linear A/B, the Phoenician alphabet, Chinese oracle bones, the Mayan script, Hangul's deliberate royal design — yet no streak app teaches the history of scripts, how they were deciphered, and what they reveal about ancient civilizations. Omniglot (world's largest writing systems reference) is web-only. Zero app captures this history in gamified format.

itunes_results.name: Duolingo
itunes_results.note: Individual language courses — no comparative language family education

itunes_results.name: uTalk Language Learning
itunes_results.note: Vocabulary-focused — no family trees, no comparative linguistics

daily_language_families_streak.gap_note: The 142 language families of the world (Indo-European, Sino-Tibetan, Afro-Asiatic, Niger-Congo, Austronesian, Dravidian, Turkic, Japonic...) and their internal relationships reveal the prehistory of human migration. No app teaches this comparative perspective: why Turkish and Mongolian share grammar but not vocabulary, why English and Farsi are cousins, why Japanese has no proven relatives. The worldbuilding community (1.1M) actively craves this for conlang creation.

itunes_results.name: Waking Up
itunes_results.note: Mindfulness/philosophy — not linguistic relativity or language-thought science

itunes_results.name: Brilliant
itunes_results.note: STEM education — no linguistics, no language and cognition content

daily_linguistic_relativity_streak.gap_note: The Sapir-Whorf hypothesis (language shapes thought), color perception across languages (Russians distinguish siniy/goluboy blue shades; English speakers don't), spatial reasoning in Hopi vs English, time conception in Aymara — linguistic relativity research is one of the most mind-bending bodies of science for educated laypeople, yet zero daily streak apps exist. The movie 'Arrival' (2016) sparked massive public interest in linguistic relativity with zero apps capitalizing on it.

itunes_results.name: Egyptian Hieroglyphs
itunes_results.note: Basic hieroglyphics reference — no decipherment history, no streak

itunes_results.name: Rosetta Stone
itunes_results.note: Modern language learning — zero ancient script content despite the brand name

daily_ancient_scripts_streak.gap_note: The decipherment of ancient scripts is among the greatest intellectual detective stories in history: Champollion cracking hieroglyphics via the Rosetta Stone (1822), Michael Ventris decoding Linear B as Mycenaean Greek (1952), the ongoing mystery of Linear A and the Indus Valley script. AskHistorians (1.5M) treats these as premium content. Rosetta Stone (a brand NAMED after the most famous decipherment) teaches zero ancient script content. Zero streak apps teach this.

itunes_results.name: Duolingo
itunes_results.note: Living languages only — PIE reconstruction unavailable

itunes_results.name: Anki Flashcards
itunes_results.note: Community decks exist for PIE but no curated streak app

daily_proto_indo_european_streak.gap_note: Proto-Indo-European is the reconstructed ancestor of languages spoken by 3.2 billion people (English, Spanish, French, German, Russian, Hindi, Bengali, Farsi...). No app teaches PIE: the kurgan hypothesis of IE origins, the Yamnaya culture, how *pṓds became 'foot/pied/Fuß/pada', the 8 cases, the reconstructed mythology (*Dyēus Ph₂tḗr → Zeus/Jupiter/Dyaus Pita). The r/history community (18.4M) regularly posts viral threads on this topic.

itunes_results.name: Duolingo
itunes_results.note: Teaches selected languages — no endangered language education content

itunes_results.name: Mango Languages
itunes_results.note: Includes some endangered languages — not a daily education streak on endangerment

daily_language_endangerment_streak.gap_note: 7,000 languages exist; half will be gone by 2100. Each language's death takes irreplaceable ecological knowledge, philosophical frameworks, and cognitive perspectives with it. The last speaker of Eyak (Marie Smith Jones, died 2008), the revival of Hebrew, the status of Welsh and Irish Gaelic — these are stories with massive cultural resonance. No streak app teaches language endangerment science, the political economy of language death, or what is lost when a language dies.

itunes_results.name: SignSchool
itunes_results.note: Teaches ASL vocabulary — not history of sign languages or sign linguistics

itunes_results.name: ASL - American Sign Language
itunes_results.note: ASL vocabulary lessons — zero historical/linguistic perspective

daily_sign_language_history_streak.gap_note: Sign languages are full natural languages with their own phonology, grammar, and regional dialects — ASL and BSL are NOT related despite both being used in English-speaking countries. The history of deaf education (Abbé de l'Épée founding deaf education in Paris, the Milan Conference of 1880 banning sign language in schools, William Stokoe proving ASL is a real language in 1960) is gripping yet untold in app form. SignSchool teaches vocabulary; nobody teaches the linguistics or history.
